target.title: fix(agents): allow empty-error-retry when replayMetadata is absent (#97877)
target.body: Fixes #97877. ## What Problem This Solves `replayMetadata.hadPotentialSideEffects` is accumulated across attempts via `observeReplayMetadata`. When a session executes tools on Turn 1 and then hits a transient provider 5xx on Turn 2 (before any tool execution in the current attempt), the cumulative flag is already `true` from Turn 1 — incorrectly blocking the empty-error-retry path even though the failed model call itself never ran tools. ## Why This Change Was Made Four changes to separate current-attempt side effects from cumulative session history: 1. **EmbeddedRunAttemptResult** (types.ts): Added optional `currentAttemptReplayMetadata` — records side effects from the current attempt only. 2. **normalizeEmbeddedRunAttemptResult** (run.ts): Computes `currentAttemptReplayMetadata` from per-attempt raw fields via `buildAttemptReplayMetadata` — the same function `attempt.ts` uses to compute `observedReplayMetadata` before accumulation. The cumulative `replayMetadata` field stays required via `resolveAttemptReplayMetadata(raw)` — no contract weakening. 3. **shouldRetrySilentErrorAssistantTurn** (incomplete-turn.ts): Prefers `currentAttemptReplayMetadata` to decide whether this specific model call had side effects, falling back to cumulative `replayMetadata` for legacy harnesses. 4. **Runner proof + gateway proof**: Test harness captures `[empty-error-retry]` via `mockedLog.warn`. Live gateway agent with local proxy demonstrates recovery from provider error after prior-turn tool execution. ## User Impact Agents recover from transient 5xx provider errors on any turn — even after prior turns executed tools. The documented `[empty-error-retry]` retry of up to 3 attempts fires when the failed model call itself produced no content, no tool calls, and no delivery side effects. ## Evidence **Behavior addressed**: Cumulative `replayMetadata.hadPotentialSideEffects` from prior turns blocks retry of a transient 5xx on a later turn where the model failed before tool execution. **Real environment tested**: Node 22.19+, local OpenClaw checkout. Two proof levels: (1) runner harness with real `runEmbeddedAgent` → `log.warn` capture, (2) live gateway agent with local proxy + real ZTE provider. **Proof 1 — Runner-level: `[empty-error-retry]` log capture** ``` node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run.empty-error-retry.test.ts --run ``` ``` Test Files 1 passed (1) Tests 23 passed (23) ``` Key assertions from the real `runEmbeddedAgent` path: ``` ✓ retry on 5xx → mockedLog.warn called with "[empty-error-retry]..." ✓ prior-turn tools + clean 5xx → mockedLog.warn called with "[empty-error-retry]..." (#97877 fix) ✓ current-turn tools → mockedLog.warn NOT called (no duplicate side effects) ``` Only `runEmbeddedAttempt` (HTTP call) is mocked; `normalizeEmbeddedRunAttemptResult`, `shouldRetrySilentErrorAssistantTurn`, retry loop, and `log.warn` are all production code. **Proof 2 — Gateway-level: Live agent recovery with real provider** Proxy intercepts chat #3 (Turn 2 first attempt) with plaintext error. Real ZTE Qwen3-235B-A22B API handles all other requests. ``` === Turn 1: run tool → cumulative side effects === [proxy] chat #1 → real API (model decides to run echo hello) [proxy] chat #2 → real API (tool result continuation) hello ← tool output [agent] run ... ended with stopReason=stop === Turn 2: provider error → retry → recovery === [proxy] chat #3 → plaintext body → unclassified → [empty-error-retry] response status=200 contentType=text/plain (not a transport error → runner retry) [proxy] chat #4 → real API (retry after [empty-error-retry]) response status=200 contentType=text/event-stream OK ← recovered reply [agent] run ... ended with stopReason=stop ``` Key observations: - Turn 1 used 2 chats (model call + tool continuation) → side effects accumulated - Turn 2 used 2 chats (plaintext error → retry → real API) instead of 1 - The plaintext body returned HTTP 200 — transport layer does NOT retry HTTP 200 - The runner-level `shouldRetrySilentErrorAssistantTurn` + `currentAttemptReplayMetadata` clean-check allowed retry despite cumulative side effects from Turn 1 - No duplicate tools or side effects — only 4 total chats for 2 turns **Behavior proof (5 scenario matrix)**: ``` [1] currentAttempt clean + cumulative dirty (PRIOR TOOLS) → retry YES ✅ [2] currentAttempt dirty + cumulative dirty (TOOLS RAN) → retry NO ✅ [3] LEGACY (no currentAttempt), cumulative dirty → retry NO ✅ [4] No metadata at all (5xx before tool subsystem) → retry YES ✅ [5] Both clean (clean session) → retry YES ✅ ``` ``` node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run.empty-error-retry.test.ts --run Test Files 1 passed (1) / Tests 23 passed (23) node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run.incomplete-turn.test.ts --run Test Files 1 passed (1) / Tests 114 passed (114) node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run.ts ... (all 5 changed files) (passed, exit 0) ``` **Observed result after fix**: The `runEmbeddedAgent` runner produces `[empty-error-retry]` logs when retrying. In the live gateway, a plaintext provider error on Turn 2 (after Turn 1's tool execution) triggers runner-level retry — the agent calls the model again and successfully recovers with `stopReason=stop`. Legacy harnesses without `currentAttemptReplayMetadata` fall back to cumulative `replayMetadata` (backward compatible, no retry when cumulative is dirty). **What was not tested**: Live E2E with a real 5xx HTTP status from the provider. The gateway proof used HTTP 200 + unparseable body (text/plain) to trigger `stopReason="error"` without transport-level retry — this exercises the exact same `unclassified` failover path that a real provider crash would take. ## Regression Test Plan - `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run.empty-error-retry.test.ts --run` — 23 passed - `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run.incomplete-turn.test.ts --run` — 114 passed - `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs` on all changed files — exit 0 ## Root Cause In `attempt.ts`, `replayMetadataFromState(observeReplayMetadata(getReplayState(), observedReplayMetadata))` merges per-attempt `observedReplayMetadata` with accumulated session-level state before returning. The resulting `rawAttempt.replayMetadata` is always cumulative — `hadPotentialSideEffects` carries the OR of all prior turns. Previously `normalizeEmbeddedRunAttemptResult` saved `raw.replayMetadata` as `currentAttemptReplayMetadata`, making both fields carry the same cumulative value — the per-attempt check was a no-op. The fix re-derives per-attempt metadata from the raw attempt's observable fields (toolMetas, messaging delivery evidence, session spawns, cron adds) using `buildAttemptReplayMetadata` — the same function `attempt.ts` uses to compute `observedReplayMetadata` before accumulation. ## AI Assistance - **AI-assisted**: Yes - **Model used**: Claude Opus 4.8 (1M context) - **Co-Authored-By**: Already in commit message
target.author: maweibin
